description:
Offered: every semester
This workshop course covers all aspects of developing, writing, and crafting articles that demonstrate effective use of story angle, genre conventions, and voice. Magazine features rely on solid but fresh research and effortless style, so the course emphasizes reading, research, and peer and self-editing. Students practice interview and research techniques and write in several major sub-genres of magazine features such as profiles, op-eds, and service pieces. The course is especially helpful to students interested in journalism, public relations, content development, or marketing. Cross-listed with COMM 3870
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200 or HON 1200
Note: Credit is not given for both ENGL 3870 and COMM 3870
Meets General Education Requirement(s): IHIP
